title: To Mrs. Irving,
content:
I dedicate these verses to one whom I hold dear,
    One who in the dark days drew in Christian kindness near
    May He who led me all my life do so and more to me
    If ever I forget the debt of love I owe to thee.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Nora Pembroke (Margaret Moran Dixon McDougall), titled "To Mrs. Irving,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
